channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Fly tipping on Hassett Road/ Barnabas Street, 27th May
item.description:
Over the bank holiday weekend, people have left clothes and other items by the bins here. Please can these be cleaned up

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hassett Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish bags and more, 27th May
item.description:
This has been here for a while, looks like a clear out that's spilled onto the pavement.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Whitworth Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Littered street and detritus in gutters, 27th May
item.description:
Littered street and detritus in gutters

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hargwyne Street, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipping, abandoned vehicle, fire risk, 27th May
item.description:
As shown in the attached photos, waste is being dumped in this area, including household rubbish and large items. There is also a white abandoned vehicle on or near the empty land, and the surrounding area appears to be neglected. The site is now attracting rats. If your officers speak to the waste collection staff or street cleaning team working in this area, they may also be able to confirm whether they have seen rats or signs of rat activity. This is becoming a serious public health concern for local residents. There is also a serious fire risk. Recently, people dumped rubbish in this area at night and set it on fire, and the fire brigade had to attend in middle of night. The photos show signs of burnt waste and further dumping. If no action is taken, this may happen again. Could you please arrange for the relevant team to inspect the site urgently and take action, including: Clearing the dumped waste, removing the abandoned white vehicle if applicable, installing clear “No fly-tipping” / warning signs in the area, considering CCTV or enforcement action to prevent further dumping and fires. I have attached photos showing the condition of the area. Kind regards,

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Milton Road, Lessness Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street lighting not working, 27th May
item.description:
Street lighting not working

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Plough Way, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple signs and bollards STILL out!, 27th May
item.description:
I have four reports open here that I reported months ago Each THREE times! To date these are still open as the signs are STILL unlit!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 286, Southborough Lane, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large street signs blocking drop curb, 27th May
item.description:
I am a wheelchair user from this area and the dropped curb is completely unusable right now. In order for me to get to my GP (Blackfriers medical practice) my only option is to go on the street, which is very dangerous. The dropped curb is blocked by large signage for over a week now.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Joan Street, Southwark, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wheelchair accessible is covered by plant overgrowth and signage, 27th May
item.description:
I am a wheelchair user from this area and the dropped curb is completely unusable right now. In order for me to get to my GP (Blackfriers medical practice) my only option is to go on the street, which is very dangerous. I have asked the people at the EV restaurant to remove the signage, and they have moved them to the side but remain in the area.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Joan Street, Southwark, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Drain Cover, 26th May
item.description:
A lorry hit the drain cover when turning into Pembury Crescent a few days ago. This caused the drain cover to lift and it is now sticking up.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 32,32A, Maylands Drive, Albany Park, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter, 26th May
item.description:
Lots of litter (mostly plastic drink bottles) on and around the roundabout. As this is outside Maylands Store, perhaps it might be useful to place a litter bin nearby. Please can this be tidied in the meantime.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on bike bunker, 26th May
item.description:
The bike bunker on Kinfauns Road has been graffitied on by 3 young boys, please remove.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kinfauns Road, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog Fouling, 26th May
item.description:
Someone keeps letting their dog foul under the tree near the larger entrance of George Downing Estate . Please clear
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cazenove Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: FixMyStreet ref: 9502311 NOT resolved, 26th May
item.description:
FixMyStreet ref: 9502311 has not been resolved. The bag has been run over and broken open and there are pieces of litter all over the street there, but the original bag is still there. It has now been there five days.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Newington Green, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unwanted Plastic Container Dumped and Being Used as Unofficial Bin, 26th May
item.description:
A large plastic container has been dumped by the side of the pavement and is being used as an unofficial bin. Can the local team please remove it. Thank you.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Southport Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet
